html:
Exclusive to:

The Celestial Portal is constructed by using the Portal Paraphernalia on the Florid Postern, and adding a Purple Moonlens and twenty Moon Rocks to it. Items can be added incrementally, and adding more than what is required will cause the excess to fall to the ground. Items committed cannot be removed.

When offered a Moon Rock Idol, the Celestial Portal will send the player back to the Character selection screen. When leaving the world, all items in the player's Inventory will be dropped automatically. Upon choosing a new character, the player will be transported back to the same world, keeping their Day count and Map exploration progress, along with any prototyped Crafting recipes and any adopted Critters.

Additionally, the Celestial Portal serves the same functions as the Florid Postern. It still marks the server's spawn point, and, if the server is set to Endless Mode, it can be used by Ghost Characters to resurrect at a cost of 25% of their maximum Health (this penalty can be reversed by using Booster Shots).

Using the Celestial Portal to switch character resets Touch Stone usage.

The Celestial Portal gives off a small amount of light at night, but is not an appropriate light source to ward off Charlie.

Tips

  • Players should not build a Celestial Portal in the Caves since it does not work as it should down there.

Trivia

  • When it was first introduced in the Celestial Update, changing characters at the Portal used to reset the player's map, day count, and crafting recipes. This is no longer the case as of update 288118.
  • Since Wickerbottom is able to craft Science Items with a lower tier Crafting Station than other Characters from the start, she never properly "learns" those recipes, thus even if the player had crafted any of those recipes, it would appear "locked" when changing from Wickerbottom to another character.
  • If Woodie offers a Moon Rock Idol to the Portal, Lucy the Axe will turn into a regular Axe when returning as a different character.
  • The 'eye' of the portal resembles the awakened Alter.
  • Each character has a unique quote before the game returns to the character selection screen.

Gallery

  • Wendy working on the Portal's construction.
  • The Three Stages of the construction.
  • WX-78 offering a Moon Rock Idol to the Portal.
  • The Celestial Portal glowing in the dark.
  • The Celestial Portal in a promotional image for its debut, from Rhymes with Play #208.
DSTDSRoGSWHam
plainText:
Exclusive to:
Celestial Portal
Object Info
Perk
Spawn point for newly joined players.
Resurrects ghosts when in Endless Mode.
Allows players to select a different character.
Structure Info
×1 ×20
Miscellaneous
"multiplayer_portal_moonrock"
“I'm sure there's some scientific explanation for this.”
–Wilson
“It's made of some kind of jerk rock I can't burn.”
–Willow
“Is strong like Wolfgang.”
–Wolfgang
“Cold and beautiful like death.”
–Wendy
“NETWORKING PORTAL”
–WX-78
“Its design is decidedly otherworldly.”
–Wickerbottom
“Wonder where it goes.”
–Woodie
“A bit ominous looking.”
–Maxwell
“It hath a glöw aböut it.”
–Wigfrid
“Gosh. It's so sparkly!”
–Webber
“It seems dangerous, but it's oddly calming.”
–Warly
“Glowy. Like Night Ball”
–Wormwood
“Wow. Can't even see the weld joints.”
–Winona
“Ooo, what delightful games we might play in there!”
–Wortox
“It made of moon stuff!”
–Wurt
“This one looks way fancier... but still can't get us home.”
–Walter
“I feel like I'm being watched...”
–Wanda
The Celestial Portal is constructed by using the Portal Paraphernalia on the Florid Postern, and adding a Purple Moonlens and twenty Moon Rocks to it. Items can be added incrementally, and adding more than what is required will cause the excess to fall to the ground. Items committed cannot be removed.
When offered a Moon Rock Idol, the Celestial Portal will send the player back to the Character selection screen. When leaving the world, all items in the player's Inventory will be dropped automatically. Upon choosing a new character, the player will be transported back to the same world, keeping their Day count and Map exploration progress, along with any prototyped Crafting recipes and any adopted Critters.
Additionally, the Celestial Portal serves the same functions as the Florid Postern. It still marks the server's spawn point, and, if the server is set to Endless Mode, it can be used by Ghost Characters to resurrect at a cost of 25% of their maximum Health (this penalty can be reversed by using Booster Shots).
Using the Celestial Portal to switch character resets Touch Stone usage.
The Celestial Portal gives off a small amount of light at night, but is not an appropriate light source to ward off Charlie.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Players should not build a Celestial Portal in the Caves since it does not work as it should down there.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
When it was first introduced in the Celestial Update, changing characters at the Portal used to reset the player's map, day count, and crafting recipes. This is no longer the case as of update 288118.
Since Wickerbottom is able to craft Science Items with a lower tier Crafting Station than other Characters from the start, she never properly "learns" those recipes, thus even if the player had crafted any of those recipes, it would appear "locked" when changing from Wickerbottom to another character.
If Woodie offers a Moon Rock Idol to the Portal, Lucy the Axe will turn into a regular Axe when returning as a different character.
The 'eye' of the portal resembles the awakened Alter.
Each character has a unique quote before the game returns to the character selection screen.
“I can see the light!”
–Wilson, before vanishing.
“A burning light!”
–Willow, before vanishing.
“Wolfgang is scared!”
–Wolfgang, before vanishing.
“Abigail?”
–Wendy, before vanishing.
“I SEE THE BLUE SCREEN!”
–WX-78, before vanishing.
“I've never read anything describing this!”
–Wickerbottom, before vanishing.
“This feels strangely familiar...”
–Woodie, before vanishing.
“Say pal, I don't look so good...”
–Maxwell, before vanishing.
“'Tis the lights öf Valhalla?”
–Wigfrid, before vanishing.
“Everything's getting fuzzy!”
–Webber, before vanishing.
“I'm going to the kitchen in the sky.”
–Warly, before vanishing.
“Don't want to go!”
–Wormwood, before vanishing.
“Good thing I've got all my affairs in order.”
–Winona, before vanishing.
“Hyuyu, fare thee well!”
–Wortox, before vanishing.
“Feel cold...”
–Wurt, before vanishing.
“I don't feel so good...”
–Walter, before vanishing.
“Wait, stop! I need more time--!”
–Wanda, before vanishing.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Wendy working on the Portal's construction.
The Three Stages of the construction.
WX-78 offering a Moon Rock Idol to the Portal.
The Celestial Portal glowing in the dark.
The Celestial Portal in a promotional image for its debut, from Rhymes with Play #208.

Like Night Ball |wurt = It made of moon stuff! |walter = This one looks way fancier... but still can't get us home. |wanda = I feel like I'm being watched... |choose = wickerbottom}} The '''Celestial Portal''' is constructed by using the [[Portal Paraphernalia]] on the [[Florid Postern]], and adding a [[Moonlens|Purple Moonlens]] and twenty [[Moon Rock]]s to it. Items can be added incrementally, and adding more than what is required will cause the excess to fall to the ground. Items committed cannot be removed. When offered a [[Moon Rock Idol]], the Celestial Portal will send the player back to the [[Characters|Character]] selection screen. When leaving the world, all items in the player's [[Inventory]] will be dropped automatically. Upon choosing a new character, the player will be transported back to the same world, keeping their [[Day-Night Cycle|Day count]] and [[Map]] exploration progress, along with any prototyped [[Crafting]] recipes and any adopted [[Critters]]. Additionally, the Celestial Portal serves the same functions as the Florid Postern. It still marks the server's spawn point, and, if the server is set to Endless Mode, it can be used by [[Ghost Characters]] to resurrect at a cost of 25% of their maximum [[Health]] (this penalty can be reversed by using [[Booster Shot]]s). Using the Celestial Portal to switch character resets [[Touch Stone/DST|Touch Stone]] usage. The Celestial Portal gives off a small amount of light at night, but is not an appropriate light source to ward off [[Charlie]]. == {{pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Players should not build a Celestial Portal in the [[Caves]] since it does not work as it should down there. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * When it was first introduced in the [[Don't Starve Together/Version History#September 27th, 2018|Celestial Update]], changing characters at the Portal used to reset the player's map, day count, and crafting recipes. This is no longer the case as of [[Don't Starve Together/Version History#October 3rd, 2018|update 288118]]. * Since [[Wickerbottom]] is able to craft [[:Category:Science|Science]] [[Items]] with a lower tier [[:Category:Crafting Stations|Crafting Station]] than other Characters from the start, she never properly "learns" those recipes, thus even if the player had crafted any of those recipes, it would appear "locked" when changing from Wickerbottom to another character. * If [[Woodie]] offers a Moon Rock Idol to the Portal, [[Lucy the Axe]] will turn into a regular [[Axe]] when returning as a different character. *The 'eye' of the portal resembles the awakened [[Them|Alter]]. * Each character has a unique quote before the game returns to the character selection screen. {{Quotes |wilson = I can see the light! |willow = A burning light! |wolfgang = Wolfgang is scared! |wendy = Abigail? |wx78 =I SEE THE BLUE SCREEN! |wickerbottom =I've never read anything describing this! |woodie =This feels strangely familiar... |maxwell =Say pal, I don't look so good... |wigfrid ='Tis the lights öf Valhalla? |webber =Everything's getting fuzzy! |winona =Good thing I've got all my affairs in order. |warly = I'm going to the kitchen in the sky. |wortox = Hyuyu, fare thee well! |wormwood = Don't want to go! |wurt = Feel cold... |walter = I don't feel so good... |wanda = Wait, stop!
